novel_features.name: Journey funnel × segment
novel_features.description: Render a step-by-step journey funnel (sent → delivered → opened → clicked → converted) for one campaign, optionally cross-cut by segment.
novel_features.rationale: The Customer.io API exposes per-campaign journey_metrics but no per-segment breakdown; the funnel × segment join only exists once deliveries and segment_members are in local SQLite.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent is asked 'what fraction of segment X completed journey Y' — the answer is one query against synced data, not two exports and a spreadsheet.

novel_features.name: Segment overlap
novel_features.description: Compute pairwise and multi-way Venn diagrams of segment memberships from the local store.
novel_features.rationale: Customer.io's UI offers no multi-segment intersection; SQL over synced segment_members produces deterministic counts and (with --show-ids) the overlapping customer IDs.
novel_features.example: customer-io segments overlap seg_19 seg_42 seg_88 --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when validating audience design — 'are my churned-risk and high-value segments overlapping?' — without exporting twice.

novel_features.name: Customer 360 timeline
novel_features.description: Chronological per-customer event stream merging identifies, deliveries, suppressions, and segment-membership events from the local store.
novel_features.rationale: The web UI scatters this across 4+ pages; only a local join can render the full timeline in one pass.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this on incident triage or 'did the welcome SMS go out to this user?' — one command instead of a UI scroll-fest.

novel_features.name: Broadcast pre-flight
novel_features.description: Before triggering a broadcast, check target segment size, suppression overlap, and last-sent recency from the local deliveries cache; emit a green/yellow/red verdict with structured reasons.
novel_features.rationale: The 1 req / 10 s broadcast throttle makes 'is this safe to send?' a real failure mode; pre-flight is the only way to answer it without spending a real broadcast slot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before any broadcast to avoid double-sending, accidental over-mailing, or 429 storms.

novel_features.name: Suppression audit
novel_features.description: Attribute every suppression in a window to the triggering bounce or complaint delivery (or 'manual' if no preceding event).
novel_features.rationale: Customer.io exposes individual suppress events but not the join to the delivery that caused them — only a local SQL join over synced suppressions × deliveries produces the audit trail.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an ops engineer needs to explain why a customer is suppressed, or when auditing complaint-driven churn.

novel_features.name: Reverse-ETL health
novel_features.description: Named verb over Reverse-ETL run history with status, row counts, error reasons, and an optional --watch poll mode.
novel_features.rationale: The official cio CLI exposes RETL only via generic api passthrough. RETL is a Premium-tier feature that ops engineers check daily — it deserves a verb.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as the daily standup question 'are warehouse syncs healthy?' — replaces three separate api calls + jq filters.

novel_features.name: Bulk suppress with audit log
novel_features.description: Read a CSV or JSONL of email/customer-id, fan out real suppress calls with adaptive throttle, append every call to a local JSONL audit log keyed by date.
novel_features.rationale: The App API takes one customer at a time and offers no audit log; the local JSONL log + adaptive throttle is the differentiator over loop-and-call from an SDK.
novel_features.example: customer-io suppressions bulk add --from-csv complaints.csv --reason complaint --dry-run
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for compliance-driven bulk actions where you need to defend later 'who got suppressed when, by whom, with what status code'.

novel_features.name: Delivery triage bundle
novel_features.description: Filter live + local deliveries by template + status + window, write a self-contained bundle (summary.md with SQL group-by error reasons, deliveries.jsonl, recipients.txt) ready to paste into an incident doc.
novel_features.rationale: Pulls live API state and joins it with the synced delivery cache; assembles the agent-shaped bundle the brief's incident persona explicitly wants.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pipe the bundle into Claude or a Notion doc for one-shot incident handoff; replaces 30+ minutes of UI scrolling.

narrative.headline: Every Customer.io action a marketer or ops engineer takes — campaigns, broadcasts, segments, deliveries, exports, suppressions, Reverse-ETL — wrapped in named verbs, backed by a local SQLite cache, and served through a bundled MCP server.
narrative.value_prop: The official customerio/cli exposes the API as raw passthrough. This CLI gives you typed commands for every meaningful workflow, plus eight commands no other tool has — journey funnels cross-cut by segment, multi-segment overlap, customer 360 timelines, broadcast pre-flight, suppression audit trails, Reverse-ETL health, bulk suppress with provenance, and incident-ready delivery triage bundles. It is also the only Customer.io tool that ships an MCP server.
narrative.auth_narrative: Customer.io uses Service Account tokens (`sa_live_*` prefix). The CLI exchanges the token for a short-lived JWT via the OAuth client-credentials endpoint at `https://us.fly.customer.io/v1/service_accounts/oauth/token` (or `eu.fly.customer.io` for EU workspaces) and uses the JWT as the Bearer for both the Journeys UI API (`/v1/...`) and the CDP control plane (`/cdp/api/...`). Run `customer-io auth login --sa-token $CIO_TOKEN --region us` once; the cached JWT auto-refreshes. The Track API (separate Site ID + API Key auth) is intentionally out of scope for v1 — the SA token is the unified credential.

quickstart.comment: One-time SA token exchange; the JWT is cached and refreshed automatically.
Confirms token, region, account_id, and which workspaces are reachable.
Populate the local SQLite cache so SQL/search/funnel/timeline work offline.
Smoke-test: list campaigns and pluck just the fields you need.
Run pre-flight before any broadcast — the 1 req / 10 s throttle is unforgiving.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `customer-io auth status`; if the JWT is expired, re-run `customer-io auth login --sa-token $CIO_TOKEN --region <us|eu>`. SA tokens themselves are long-lived; only the minted JWT expires.

troubleshoots.symptom: Calls go to the wrong region
troubleshoots.fix: Pass `--region eu` (or set `CUSTOMERIO_REGION=eu`) — both the JWT exchange and the API calls must target the same region.

troubleshoots.symptom: 429 Too Many Requests on broadcast trigger
troubleshoots.fix: Broadcast triggers are limited to 1 request per 10 seconds. Use `customer-io broadcasts preflight` first; the trigger command itself adapts to the limit. Don't wrap it in retry-without-backoff loops.
Run `customer-io sync --resources segment_members,deliveries` first. Some commands read from the local store rather than the live API.
Reverse-ETL is a Premium-tier feature; the endpoint is reachable but returns 403 on Essentials accounts. List the workspaces visible to your Service Account with `customer-io-pp-cli workspaces`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when you need to do anything in Customer.io that's tedious in the web UI: bulk suppressions with an audit trail, journey funnels cross-cut by segment, customer-360 timelines for incident triage, broadcast pre-flight before a high-stakes send, or Reverse-ETL health checks. It is also the right tool when an agent needs to drive Customer.io — the bundled MCP server exposes the same verbs over stdio and HTTP transports. For raw event ingestion (the Track API) or per-source CDP write keys, prefer one of the official server-side SDKs.

recipes.title: Find which high-value customers haven't engaged with the welcome journey
recipes.explanation: The funnel command joins synced deliveries with segment members; --agent + --select narrows the deeply-nested funnel response to just the counts and the non-engaged recipient list, keeping the agent's context lean.

recipes.title: Draft an incident bundle for a transactional bounce spike
recipes.explanation: Writes summary.md with grouped error reasons, deliveries.jsonl with full delivery objects, and recipients.txt — paste-ready for a Notion incident doc or a Claude summarize prompt.

recipes.title: Prove that a broadcast is safe to trigger
recipes.explanation: Returns a green/yellow/red verdict with structured reasons (segment size, suppression overlap, last-sent recency from synced deliveries). Run before every broadcast at non-trivial scale.

recipes.title: Bulk-suppress a complaint list with provenance
recipes.command: customer-io suppressions bulk add --from-csv complaints-2026-05.csv --reason complaint --dry-run
recipes.explanation: Drop --dry-run to commit. Every call lands in `~/.customer-io/audit/suppressions-2026-05-07.jsonl` with timestamp, recipient, status, and HTTP code — defensible later.

recipes.title: Watch Reverse-ETL syncs in real time
recipes.explanation: Polls every 60 s and prints a status row per sync; pipe to `tee` to keep a log.

trigger_phrases: what fraction of segment X opened journey Y in customer.io
